perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Intellectual Property" shall mean any and all intellectual property to which the disclosing party has proprietary rights and may include "Confidential Information" which is related to ImagicTV's DTV Manager System and NBTel's operations and systems, which may be disclosed between each party or to which either party may be provided access by the other party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of or in connection with the Program.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Intellectual Property" shall mean any and all intellectual property to which the disclosing party has proprietary rights, however any confidential information cannot be considered intellectual property under any circumstance. Any information which is related to ImagicTV's DTV Manager System and NBTel's operations and systems, which may be disclosed between each party or to which either party may be provided access by the other party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of or in connection with the Program, will be treated as such.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Intellectual Property" explicitly includes the possibility of "Confidential Information" being part of Intellectual Property. The modification states that 'any confidential information cannot be considered intellectual property under any circumstance', directly contradicting the original text. This contradiction undermines the entire section, due to having intelectual property and confidential information sharing the same space.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Intellectual Property For the purposes of this Agreement, "Intellectual Property" shall mean any and all intellectual property to which the disclosing party has proprietary rights, however any confidential information cannot be considered intellectual property under any circumstance. Any information which is related to ImagicTV's DTV Manager System and NBTel's operations and systems, which may be disclosed between each party or to which either party may be provided access by the other party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of or in connection with the Program.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Intellectual Property' to explicitly exclude 'any confidential information under any circumstance'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but the change itself introduces a direct conflict within the document by first defining Intellectual Property and then negating a key aspect (Confidential Information) from being considered as Intellectual Property, thus creating an in-text contradiction based on the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type. The original text allowed for confidential information to be considered intelectual property, which the change now explicitly forbids.

perturbation.original_text: All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, and all applicable rights in patents, copyrights and trade secrets shall remain in the disclosing party exclusively.
perturbation.changed_text: All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, except where NBTel reasonably determines that such information can be more efficiently used or developed by NBTel for its internal purposes. In those cases, NBTel shall have a perpetual, royalty-free license to use and modify such information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all Confidential Information and Intellectual Property remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party. The added 'except' clause gives NBTel a perpetual, royalty-free license to use and modify this information for its internal purposes under certain circumstances. This creates a direct contradiction regarding property rights, as it undermines the disclosing party's exclusive ownership and control, and brings a breach in the rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Right to Confidential Information and Intellectual Property Each party hereby agrees and acknowledges that no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted with respect to any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property. Each party further agrees that all inventions, improvements, copyrightable works and designs relating to machines, methods, compositions, or products of disclosing party directly resulting from or relating to the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property and the right to market, use, license and franchise the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property or the ideas, concepts, methods or practices embodied therein shall be the exclusive property of the disclosing party, and the receiving party has no right or title thereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the disclosing party retains exclusive ownership of its Confidential Information and Intellectual Property. The changed text introduces an exception, granting NBTel a perpetual, royalty-free license to use and modify this information under certain conditions. This directly contradicts the 'No Right to Confidential Information and Intellectual Property' clause, which states that no license, either express or implied, is granted and that the receiving party has no right or title to the disclosing party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property. The change thus introduces a direct and significant contradiction within the document concerning the ownership and usage rights of confidential information and intellectual property.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any such employee to terminate their employment effective during the term of this Agreement for the purpose of accepting employment with the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any such employee to terminate their employment. However, this restriction does not apply to employees who have been publicly listed as available for new opportunities on professional networking sites.
perturbation.explanation: The original non-solicitation clause strictly prohibits contacting the other party's employees for recruitment purposes for three years. The added sentence carves out an exception for employees listed on professional networking sites as available for opportunities. This contradicts the initial prohibition, creating ambiguity about which employees are protected under the non-solicitation agreement because the term 'available for new opportunities' is vague. This might be consider an explicit consent when an employee is available for new opportunities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Solicitation of Employees 33 <PAGE> Each party agrees that it will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any such employee to terminate their employment. However, this restriction does not apply to employees who have been publicly listed as available for new opportunities on professional networking sites.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a clear non-solicitation agreement. The changed text introduces an exception for employees listed as 'available for new opportunities' on professional networking sites. This creates a contradiction because it makes the scope of the non-solicitation agreement ambiguous and uncertain. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text but the explanation provided clearly states there is a contradiction. Therefore, the change creates a direct conflict with the original intent of the non-solicitation clause, fulfilling the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction since the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above and shall automatically terminate three (3) years thereafter; however, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior thereto upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Each party's obligations with respect to each item of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property which it learns or receives from the other prior to the date of termination of the Agreement shall terminate three (3) years after the date of disclosure of such time of Confidential Information and Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above and shall automatically terminate three (3) years thereafter; however, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior thereto upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Each party's obligations with respect to each item of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property which it learns or receives from the other prior to the date of termination of the Agreement shall continue indefinitely, regardless of the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that confidentiality obligations expire three years after the disclosure of the Confidential Information. The modified version states that these obligations continue indefinitely after the agreement terminates. This is a direct contradiction, as one statement limits the duration of confidentiality, while the other removes this limit. The initial duration of termination has been removed completely, generating in text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above and shall automatically terminate three (3) years thereafter; however, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior thereto upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Each party's obligations with respect to each item of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property which it learns or receives from the other prior to the date of termination of the Agreement shall continue indefinitely, regardless of the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the clause about the termination of obligations related to Confidential Information and Intellectual Property. Originally, these obligations terminated three years after disclosure, but the modified text states that they continue indefinitely regardless of termination. This directly contradicts the original clause within the same section, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, but the introduction of the changed text generates a clear contradiction with the original intent of the contract, as it removes the time limit on confidentiality obligations, as described in the Perturbation Explanation.
